skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem: In the xy-plane, the graph of y = x2 − 16 and line k intersect at the points (2, a) and (6, b), where a and b are constants. What is the slope of line k?
rationale: Substituting x = 2 gives a = 22 − 16 = −12. Substituting x = 6 gives b = 62 − 16 = 20. The slope of line k is (b − a)/(6 − 2) = (20 − (−12))/4 = 32/4 = 8.
